notes: Meta-Harness self-optimization loop policy. A proposer coding agent reads RAW execution traces (not summaries) + prior candidate bundles, rewrites the CONTENT surfaces listed in mutable_surfaces, and the loop benchmarks each candidate to build a Pareto frontier (accuracy vs context-token cost). MetaHarnessCoordinator (mChatAI+ Swift, DEBUG-only during soak) reads this. ALL policy here — no Swift constants (RULE #1). Tunable via PR to mchatai-source + refreshMchataisourceCache, no rebuild. Double-lock: enabled=true AND FeatureFlag metaHarnessEnabled=true are BOTH required for any non-dry-run campaign to fire. Either gate alone is a no-op.
_mutable_surfaces_notes: Paths ending in '/' are directory prefixes (every file beneath is in-scope); others are single files. v1 = the paper's 'state construction' surfaces only. Generalize to wizard/prompts/*, harness/prompts/*, harness/routing/*, wisdom/packs/* in a later phase (each addition needs a schema-decode check in MetaHarnessBundleValidator + an invalidation hook in MetaHarnessOverlayService.invalidateContentCaches()).

budget._notes: Campaign cost = baseline benchmark + iterations*candidates_per_iteration*(proposer run + benchmark batch). campaign_usd_cap is a hard ceiling enforced from DebugTunnelWizardResult.estimatedCostUSD + proposer CLI spend; the loop breaks when reached. proposer_token_budget bounds a single proposer CLI run.

benchmark._notes: The coordinator fires DebugTunnelWizardResult-returning tunnelRunWizard in-process (same path SelfLearnCoordinator uses), so sessionID + inputTokens + estimatedCostUSD come back directly — no Python shell-out. holdout_fraction reserves a deterministic slice (entries flagged holdout:true, else every-Nth by index) used ONLY by the keep-bar for PR-eligibility; the proposer never sees benchmark cases (it reads the trace corpus + prior candidate bundles). Train metrics drive the frontier shown to the proposer; holdout metrics gate the PR.

scoring._notes: accuracy = land_rate (renderQuality=='playable' fraction joined from landing-ledger.jsonl by sessionID), tiebreak mean rubricScore/14 from rubric-ledger.jsonl. cost = mean DebugTunnelWizardResult.inputTokens (≈ context tokens fed to the model). A candidate with fewer than min_samples_for_score scored sessions is not placed on the frontier.

keep_bar._notes: A candidate is PR-eligible iff it Pareto-non-dominates-or-matches baseline on the HELD-OUT split AND does not regress land_rate beyond epsilon_land_rate. Lack of held-out coverage for a genre is NOT an auto-reject (RULE #3) — it is surfaced as a coverage-gap flag on the PR.

proposer._invocation_notes: Print-mode CLI invocation run via mChatAIShell (unsandboxed) from the candidate dir as cwd. {PROMPT_FILE} = the composed proposer prompt written into the candidate dir; {ADD_DIRS} = --add-dir flags granting read access to the staged trace corpus + archive. Print mode (-p) is used instead of the agentic stream-json conductor because the proposer is a one-shot 'read traces, edit content files, write proposal.json' task. Swap 'sonnet' -> 'opus' here (zero rebuild) for higher-quality proposals at more cost. RULE #1: the CLI specifics live in content, not the Swift binary.
proposer._notes: The proposer is a Claude Code CLI run (WizardCLIRunner) given --add-dir access to (a) its candidate bundle dir [RW] and (b) the read-only trace corpus + archive of prior candidates. Its instructions live in instructions_path (RULE #1 — the proposer's brain is hot-patchable content). It must edit ONLY files under its candidate dir; a deterministic post-run write-scope validator enforces this.

apply._notes: Ship posture: auto-open ONE draft PR per PR-eligible frontier knee (cost-optimal / balanced / quality-optimal), each labeled with its operating point + benchmark/cost deltas + the proposer's hypothesis. require_merge:true means a HUMAN merges the chosen operating point — the loop never merges. Gated by FeatureFlag metaHarnessAutoPR (default OFF); a dry-run (planned PR, no git) always works regardless of the flag.
